description: Canonical list of GHL workflow action and trigger type strings verified against the live internal API. Single source of truth — all consumers (ghl-mcp TypeScript, ghl-superspeed-v3 Python, docs) must read from this file. Update here, then run scripts/sync-types.sh to propagate.

sources.actions: Verified via GHL save API (action save validates strictly; 'corrupted type' 400 on mismatch). Confirmed in /Users/Naegele/dev/ghl-superspeed-v3/lib/engine.py VERIFIED_ACTIONS frozenset.
sources.triggers: Extracted from GHL workflow builder JS bundle (client-app-automation-workflows.leadconnectorhq.com). Documented in /Users/Naegele/dev/ghl-automation-builder/schemas/trigger-schemas.md. Trigger API does NOT validate type strings at save time — validation happens at execution.

criticalGotchas.rule: PUT requests on workflows require an exact `version` field match. GHL increments on each save. Stale versions return 400.
Action save API validates type strings strictly. Invalid types return 'corrupted type' 400. Use only types in actions.types.
Trigger save API does NOT validate type strings — validation happens at execution. Use triggers.types to be safe.
Wait step unit must be `hour` (singular), `minutes` (plural), `days` (plural). `hours` (plural) renders value but not unit label.
After POST creating a trigger, PUT update it with `targetActionId` pointing to the first step ID. Without this, the trigger floats disconnected.
Location tags must be created via POST /workflow/{loc}/tags/create BEFORE a trigger references them. Otherwise the tag renders blank.
PUT /workflow/{loc}/{wfId}/auto-save syncs steps and triggers to Firebase Storage. Required for advanced canvas to render the workflow.
Triggers created via API stored in MongoDB but NOT immediately synced to Firebase Storage. get_triggers reads from Firebase, so new triggers invisible until full save/publish cycle.
